title: Briar Rose (Sleeping Beauty)
lines: Once a king and queen had a daughter so beautiful that they gave a great celebration for her christening.
All the wise women in the kingdom were invited to bestow gifts upon the child.
But there was one old witch who had not been invited, and she was very angry.
In revenge, she cursed the princess, saying that on her fifteenth birthday she would prick her finger on a spindle and fall into a sleep that would last for a hundred years.
A good fairy, who had not yet given her gift, softened the curse by saying that a prince would come and wake her with a kiss.
The king, trying to prevent the curse, ordered all spindles in the kingdom to be burned.
On her fifteenth birthday, the princess was wandering in the castle when she found an old woman spinning.
The princess had never seen a spindle before and reached out to touch it.
She pricked her finger and immediately fell into a deep sleep.
Her parents, heartbroken, laid her in a beautiful bed in a tower.
The good fairy cast a spell so that all the people in the castle fell asleep as well.
A hedge of thorns grew up around the castle, protecting the sleeping princess.
For many years, the castle remained hidden behind the thorn hedge.
Many princes tried to force their way through the hedge but failed.
After a hundred years, a prince came who was brave and kind.
As he approached the hedge, the thorns parted to let him pass.
He made his way to the tower and found the sleeping princess.
He was so struck by her beauty that he leaned down and gently kissed her.
At the touch of his lips, the princess woke up.
The spell was broken, and all the people in the castle woke from their sleep.
The king and queen welcomed the prince with great joy.
The princess and the prince were married and lived happily ever after.
